name: search
description: Enterprise search engine. Thin wrapper around `laravel/scout` with our conventions: attribute-driven registration (#[Searchable] / #[SearchField] / #[SearchFacet] / #[SearchSynonym] / #[SearchBoost]), pluggable engine adapters (Meilisearch default for user-facing, Postgres FTS for internal, pgvector for semantic, Elasticsearch stub for v2), a unified query API (`Search::query('term')->across(Model::class, ...)->boost(...)->filter(...)->get()`) with an engine-native escape hatch (`Search::engine('meilisearch')->raw($body)`), zero-downtime reindex via alias swap, per-tenant + per-language synonym registry, saved queries + smart lists, user-facing autocomplete (`GET /search/suggest`), and full analytics (query volume, no-results, click-through). Every long-running sync produces a persisted `search_sync_jobs` domain record with notifications on finish. Foundation's earlier `Searchable` trait + `->searchable()` blueprint macro + `IndexSearchableJob` + `search:reindex`/`search:flush` commands + `SearchEngineResolver` binding moved here on module inception; foundation retains no search mentions.

wraps.rationale: Scout is Laravel's canonical search abstraction with a mature driver ecosystem. We stand on top: `AbstractSearchableModel` wraps Scout's `Searchable` trait and layers attribute-driven registration (`#[Searchable]`, `#[SearchField]`, ...), our SearchIndex + SearchSyncJob + SearchSynonym + SearchSavedQuery + SearchAnalyticsEvent domain records, a unified `Search::query()` cross-model API with per-engine escape hatch, and per-tenant synonym / saved-query registries. Scout's `->searchable()` push, chunked backfill, `->makeAllSearchable()`, `Scout::searchUsing()`, and driver contract are used verbatim — we never re-implement queueing or driver-swap logic.

engineAdapters.description: Concrete Scout drivers we wire in. Each is a Scout-native package we consume; our `EngineRegistry` catalogues them and matches per-model config against the adapter.

adapters.role: default for internal-only indexes (audit, activity, admin logs) — no new infrastructure
adapters.notes: No typo tolerance (add pg_trgm for fuzzy). Prefer for internal search where results ship in-tenant to admins.

adapters.role: semantic similarity (v1 stub — activated when the first AI feature lands)
adapters.notes: Requires the pgvector Postgres extension. Same treatment as foundation's `HasVectorEmbeddings` — the code is here but the feature is gated by `search.pgvector` feature toggle + `search.can_use_pgvector` entitlement.

adapters.role: future — for the 100M+ document tier or regulated markets that require ES specifically
supports: everything Meilisearch does + complex aggregations + arbitrary DSL
adapters.notes: The engine class is a no-op that throws SEARCH_ENGINE_NOT_ENABLED. Present so `EngineRegistry` documents the future path + so migration effort is a swap of the adapter, not a rewrite.

foundation.reason: Foundation is pure infrastructure with zero domain entities. Search is a data-domain in its own right — it owns entities (search_indexes, search_sync_jobs, search_synonyms, search_saved_queries, search_analytics_events), engine adapters, HTTP surface, notifications, retention policies, and third-party wrap semantics (`laravel/scout`). Same rationale as splitting `transfer` / `activity` / `audit` off from foundation instead of embedding them there.

tenant.description: Tenant surface: run queries + autocomplete + view analytics + manage synonyms + manage saved queries + inspect + cancel own sync jobs.

central_admin.description: Platform-admin cross-tenant view + operations: reindex any index, flush indexes, view engine health, cross-tenant analytics, cross-tenant synonym governance.

reuses.notifications: Every long-running sync job completion / failure produces a Laravel Notification via the notifications module. Channels (mail / broadcast / database / push) resolved once at dispatch from search_sync_jobs.notify_channels — same pattern as transfer.
reuses.activity: Every search_sync_jobs status transition + synonym mutation + saved query CRUD writes an activity row via HasActivityLog. Tenant admins see 'Alice reindexed the athletes index' + 'Bob added synonym flu ↔ influenza' in the feed.
reuses.audit: Every reindex / flush / synonym mutation writes an audit row via HasAudit. Compliance surface distinct from the product feed.
reuses.transfer: Optional — the `search:reindex --from-export` mode consumes an xfer_artifact CSV / JSON as the source of truth instead of live DB, useful for cross-region migrations. Not required for normal operation.
